    base ¶

    Classes:

    • BinaryMetric –

      Mother class for all binary classification metrics.

    • ClassificationMetric –

      Mother class for all classification metrics.

    • Metric –

      Mother class for all metrics.

    • Metrics –

      A container class for handling multiple metrics at once.

    • MultiClassMetric –

      Mother class for all multi-class classification metrics.

    • RegressionMetric –

      Mother class for all regression metrics.

    • WrapperMetric –

    BinaryMetric ¶

    BinaryMetric(cm=None, pos_val=True)

    Bases: ClassificationMetric

    
              flowchart TD
              vendor.river.river.metrics.base.BinaryMetric[BinaryMetric]
              vendor.river.river.metrics.base.ClassificationMetric[ClassificationMetric]
              vendor.river.river.metrics.base.Metric[Metric]

                              vendor.river.river.metrics.base.ClassificationMetric --> vendor.river.river.metrics.base.BinaryMetric
                                vendor.river.river.metrics.base.Metric --> vendor.river.river.metrics.base.ClassificationMetric
                



              click vendor.river.river.metrics.base.BinaryMetric href "" "vendor.river.river.metrics.base.BinaryMetric"
              click vendor.river.river.metrics.base.ClassificationMetric href "" "vendor.river.river.metrics.base.ClassificationMetric"
              click vendor.river.river.metrics.base.Metric href "" "vendor.river.river.metrics.base.Metric"

    Mother class for all binary classification metrics.

    Parameters¶

    cm This parameter allows sharing the same confusion matrix between multiple metrics. Sharing a confusion matrix reduces the amount of storage and computation time. pos_val Value to treat as "positive".

    Methods:

    • get –

      Return the current value of the metric.

    • is_better_than –

      Indicate if the current metric is better than another one.

    Attributes:

    • requires_labels –

      Indicates if labels are required, rather than probabilities.

    • works_with_weights (bool) –

      Indicate whether the model takes into consideration the effect of sample weights

    requires_labels property ¶

    requires_labels

    Indicates if labels are required, rather than probabilities.

    works_with_weights property ¶

    works_with_weights: bool

    Indicate whether the model takes into consideration the effect of sample weights

    get abstractmethod ¶

    get() -> float

    Return the current value of the metric.

    is_better_than ¶

    is_better_than(other) -> bool

    Indicate if the current metric is better than another one.

    ClassificationMetric ¶

    ClassificationMetric(cm=None)

    Bases: Metric

    
              flowchart TD
              vendor.river.river.metrics.base.ClassificationMetric[ClassificationMetric]
              vendor.river.river.metrics.base.Metric[Metric]

                              vendor.river.river.metrics.base.Metric --> vendor.river.river.metrics.base.ClassificationMetric
                


              click vendor.river.river.metrics.base.ClassificationMetric href "" "vendor.river.river.metrics.base.ClassificationMetric"
              click vendor.river.river.metrics.base.Metric href "" "vendor.river.river.metrics.base.Metric"

    Mother class for all classification metrics.

    Parameters¶

    cm This parameter allows sharing the same confusion matrix between multiple metrics. Sharing a confusion matrix reduces the amount of storage and computation time.

    Methods:

    • get –

      Return the current value of the metric.

    • is_better_than –

      Indicate if the current metric is better than another one.

    Attributes:

    • requires_labels –

      Indicates if labels are required, rather than probabilities.

    • works_with_weights (bool) –

      Indicate whether the model takes into consideration the effect of sample weights

    requires_labels property ¶

    requires_labels

    Indicates if labels are required, rather than probabilities.

    works_with_weights property ¶

    works_with_weights: bool

    Indicate whether the model takes into consideration the effect of sample weights

    get abstractmethod ¶

    get() -> float

    Return the current value of the metric.

    is_better_than ¶

    is_better_than(other) -> bool

    Indicate if the current metric is better than another one.

    ClusteringMetric ¶

    Bases: Base, ABC

    
              flowchart TD
              vendor.river.river.metrics.base.ClusteringMetric[ClusteringMetric]

              

              click vendor.river.river.metrics.base.ClusteringMetric href "" "vendor.river.river.metrics.base.ClusteringMetric"

    Mother class of all internal clustering metrics.

    Methods:

    • get –

      Return the current value of the metric.

    • revert –

      Revert the metric.

    • update –

      Update the metric.

    • works_with –

      Indicates whether or not a metric can work with a given model.

    Attributes:

    • bigger_is_better (bool) –

      Indicates if a high value is better than a low one or not.

    bigger_is_better abstractmethod property ¶

    bigger_is_better: bool

    Indicates if a high value is better than a low one or not.

    get abstractmethod ¶

    get() -> float

    Return the current value of the metric.

    revert abstractmethod ¶

    revert(x, y_pred, centers, w=1.0) -> None

    Revert the metric.

    update abstractmethod ¶

    update(x, y_pred, centers, w=1.0) -> None

    Update the metric.

    works_with ¶

    works_with(model: Estimator) -> bool

    Indicates whether or not a metric can work with a given model.

    MeanMetric ¶

    MeanMetric()

    Bases: ABC

    
              flowchart TD
              vendor.river.river.metrics.base.MeanMetric[MeanMetric]

              

              click vendor.river.river.metrics.base.MeanMetric href "" "vendor.river.river.metrics.base.MeanMetric"

    Many metrics are just running averages. This is a utility class that avoids repeating tedious stuff throughout the module for such metrics.

    Metric ¶

    Bases: Base, ABC

              vendor.river.river.metrics.base.Metric[Metric]

              

              click vendor.river.river.metrics.base.Metric href "" "vendor.river.river.metrics.base.Metric"

    Mother class for all metrics.

    Methods:

    • get –

      Return the current value of the metric.

    • is_better_than –

      Indicate if the current metric is better than another one.

    • revert –

      Revert the metric.

    • update –

      Update the metric.

    • works_with –

      Indicates whether or not a metric can work with a given model.

    Attributes:

    • bigger_is_better (bool) –

      Indicate if a high value is better than a low one or not.

    • works_with_weights (bool) –

      Indicate whether the model takes into consideration the effect of sample weights

    bigger_is_better abstractmethod property ¶

    bigger_is_better: bool

    Indicate if a high value is better than a low one or not.

    works_with_weights property ¶

    works_with_weights: bool

    Indicate whether the model takes into consideration the effect of sample weights

    get abstractmethod ¶

    get() -> float

    Return the current value of the metric.

    is_better_than ¶

    is_better_than(other) -> bool

    Indicate if the current metric is better than another one.

    revert abstractmethod ¶

    revert(y_true, y_pred) -> None

    Revert the metric.

    update abstractmethod ¶

    update(y_true, y_pred) -> None

    Update the metric.

    works_with abstractmethod ¶

    works_with(model: Estimator) -> bool

    Indicates whether or not a metric can work with a given model.

    A container class for handling multiple metrics at once.

    Mother class for all multi-class classification metrics.

    Parameters¶

    cm This parameter allows sharing the same confusion matrix between multiple metrics. Sharing a confusion matrix reduces the amount of storage and computation time.

    Methods:

    • get –

      Return the current value of the metric.

    • is_better_than –

      Indicate if the current metric is better than another one.

    Attributes:

    • requires_labels –

      Indicates if labels are required, rather than probabilities.

    • works_with_weights (bool) –

      Indicate whether the model takes into consideration the effect of sample weights
